content:
Hi all,
I made a single-page, web-based app partially to create a useful tool and partly as a demo of a couple JS libraries.
The app allows you to create and run multiple stopwatches at one time. A feature to note is the ability to label each stopwatch. The app is static and data is persisted to browser localstorage with no backend. The app would be free (no ads, etc.) and open source, hosted with GitHub Pages.
My question is regarding privacy regulations, particularly the GDPR. The app does not explicitly ask for personal data such as name, email, etc. However, more implicit data such as IP address, device identifier, etc. would obviously be processed by GitHub. Also it's possible the user could label the stopwatch with personal data (since they can enter anything they want). Would either of these concerns warrant a privacy disclaimer/policy? If so, do you think it would be fine to just include one in the repo?
I may be overthinking all this, but I'd appreciate if anyone has any advice. Thanks!

  submitted by   /u/osmium_2259
[link]   [comments]
